meta.name: Half Intermediate
meta.subtitle: Break 1:45 — 5:00/km for 21.1km
meta.description: For runners with a solid base who want to break 1:45 for the half marathon. Twenty weeks of threshold-focused training, weekly track sessions and long runs building to 22km. Pfitzinger-influenced in structure.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-1:45 requires 5:00/km for 21km. The threshold runs are the engine of this plan — they directly build your ability to sustain 5:00/km. The long runs build the endurance to sustain it to the line. Do not sacrifice the long run for extra midweek volume.

weeks.title: Base assessment
weeks.note: First week. Tuesday assesses your interval fitness; Thursday establishes your threshold feel.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 5x1km @ current HM pace (2min rec) · 2km CD · 11km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Goblet squat 2x12 · Romanian deadlift 2x12 · Reverse lunge 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first lower body strength session. Keep loads light — this week is about learning the movements. Lower body strength is the foundation of running economy.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 22min @ threshold (4:50–5:00/km) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 2x12 · DB row 2x12 · Push-ups 2x12 · Face pulls 2x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first upper body session. Focus on form, not load. Upper body and core strength directly improves your running posture and arm drive efficiency.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 9km easy · With strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
14km easy · 5:55–6:20/km — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Building
weeks.note: Second week. The 800m reps develop race-pace sharpness.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 7x800m @ HM pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 11km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 25min @ threshold · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 10km easy · With strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
16km easy · 5:55–6:20/km — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate recovery week. Volume drops. The threshold and interval adaptations from the previous three weeks consolidate now.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 6km easy · No intensity — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

weeks.title: Race simulation
weeks.note: 10km at race pace on Tuesday — your most specific training stimulus.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:15/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 10km @ 5:00/km · 2km CD · 14km — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 2km WU · 2x16min @ threshold (90sec rec) · 2km CD — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

2km WU · 3x800m @ race pace · 2km CD · 6km — Sharp intervals close to race week keep your legs feeling quick without adding volume. The fitness is already built — this is a reminder, not a workout.

weeks.title: Race week — sub-1:45 attempt
weeks.note: Race week.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 3km easy · 4x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 3km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 2km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 4x100m strides · Race: through 5km at 5:02, settle to 5:00, build from 15km · Gel at 7km and 14km · Negative split target — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey, or two slices of toast with peanut butter. 600ml water. Stick entirely to foods you have eaten before long runs — half marathon nutrition starts with breakfast.
300ml water or isotonic drink. Do not gulp — sip steadily. If you normally use caffeine, this is the time.
10–15min easy jog. 4x100m strides. The warm-up is essential for a half marathon — you will not be running yourself into it in the first mile.
Gel with water at the mile 6 or 7 station. Take it with a full cup of water, not a sip. If you have not trained with gels, take water only.
Second gel at mile 10 if you trained with two gels. Otherwise water only. Do not take a gel you have not practised with.
Water or isotonic drink at the finish line. 500ml in the first 10 minutes. Keep moving — walk for at least 10 minutes to flush lactic acid.
Banana and protein bar or shake. Start the recovery process immediately — you have just run 21km.
Within 90 minutes: protein (30–40g), carbohydrates, vegetables. 1.5g protein per kg bodyweight today. Drink consistently through the afternoon — 500ml extra per hour for the first 3 hours.
